---
title: 'Discovery | D.I.S.C'
date: '2014-10-20T05:49:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3315834151089186657'
blogger_filename: '/2014/10/discovery-disc.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Discovery'
---

<br /><h4>In the D.I.S.C inventory: I am a <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/conscientiousness/" target="_blank">C -Conscientiousness.</a></h4>Take the DISC assessment <a href="http://discpersonalitytesting.com/free-disc-test/" target="_blank">here</a>.  The disc assessment divides the personality traits into Outgoing (D/I)  and Reserved (S/C) and then divides again into People Driven (I/S) and  Task Driven (D/C). All people will likely score some in each of the four  quadrants. The test then shows "The Real You" and the "The way you  behave".<br /><br />I show a strong preference toward the C in  both inward tendencies, and outward behaviors. I also show some leaning  in the D/S, but VERY little in the I. <br /><br />People with the C style place an<b> emphasis on working conscientiously within existing circumstances to ensure quality and accuracy</b>.<br /><br />People with the D style place an <b>emphasis on shaping the environment by overcoming opposition to accomplish results.</b><br /><br />People with the S style place an<b> emphasis on cooperating with others within existing circumstances to carry out the task.</b>.<br /><br />People with the i style place an <b>emphasis on shaping the environment by influencing or persuading others.</b><br /><br />Another test showed me High in C on both graphs, and higher in I internally than D or S. Huh?! See bottom. <br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/disc-6e35f0b3af4a.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/disc-6e35f0b3af4a.png" height="190" width="400" /></a></div><br /><br /><h2> What do the letters mean?</h2><table border="0" cellpadding="5" cellspacing="5" style="width: 100%;"><tbody><tr>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8; border-top: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/dominance/"><img alt="D" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/D.png.aspx" style="height: 101px; width: 100px;" title="D" /></a></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8; border-top: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><b>Dominance</b><br /><i>Person places emphasis on accomplishing results, the bottom line, confidence</i></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8; border-top: 1px solid #e0ddd8; width: 150px;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><b>Behaviors</b></span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Sees the big picture</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Can be blunt</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Accepts challenges</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Gets straight to the point<br />    <span style="font-size: 12px;"><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/dominance/">Learn more</a></span></span></td>  </tr><tr>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><img alt="I" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/I.png.aspx" style="height: 101px; width: 100px;" title="I" /></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><b>Influence</b><br /><i>Person places emphasis on influencing or persuading others, openness, relationships</i></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><b>Behaviors</b></span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Shows enthusiasm</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Is optimistic</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Likes to collaborate</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Dislikes being ignored<br />    <span style="font-size: 12px;"><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/influence/">Learn more</a></span></span></td>  </tr><tr>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><img alt="S" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/S.png.aspx" style="height: 101px; width: 100px;" title="S" /></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><b>Steadiness</b><br /><i>Person places emphasis on cooperation, sincerity, dependability</i></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><b>Behaviors</b></span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Doesn't like to be rushed</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Calm manner</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Calm approach</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Supportive actions</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Humility<br />    <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/steadiness/">Learn more</a></span></td>  </tr><tr>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><img alt="C" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/C.png.aspx" style="height: 101px; width: 100px;" title="C" /></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><b>Conscientiousness</b><br /><i>Person places emphasis on quality and accuracy, expertise, competency</i></td>   <td style="border-bottom: 1px solid #e0ddd8;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><b>Behaviors</b></span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Enjoys independence</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Objective reasoning</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Wants the details</span><br /><span style="font-size: 12px;">- Fears being wrong<br />    <span style="font-size: 12px;"><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/conscientiousness/">Learn more</a></span></span></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><h3><b><br /></b></h3><h3><b>I tested highest on the C, by a long shot, second highest was D for my internal, S medium, I VERY LOW. </b></h3><br /><h1>DiSC Conscientiousness (C) Profile Overview</h1><h2> <img alt="Conscientiousness (C)" src="https://www.discprofile.com/DiscProfile/media/Images/Other/D-i-S-C/C-DiSC.png" style="float: right;" />DiSC profiles explained: C is for Conscientiousness</h2>If you've recently taken the DiSC<sup>Â®</sup> profile, your report has a  more personalized explanation of what having a C style means for you.  But this can be a useful page to link to and have others learn a bit  more about you. Or you can use it to better understand someone you work  or live with.<br /><br />People with the C style place an<b> emphasis on working conscientiously within existing circumstances to ensure quality and accuracy</b>.<br /><br />A person with a C style<br /><ul><li>  is motivated by opportunities to gain knowledge, showing their expertise, and quality work.</li><li>  prioritizes ensuring accuracy, maintaining stability, and challenging assumptions.</li><li>  is described as careful, cautious, systematic, diplomatic, accurate and tactful.</li><li>  may be limited by being overcritical, overanalyzing and isolating themselves.</li><li>  may fear criticism and being wrong.</li><li>  values quality and accuracy</li></ul>Goals:<br /><ul><li>  unique accomplishments</li><li>  correctness</li><li>  stability</li><li>  predictable accomplishments</li><li>  personal growth</li></ul>Will need to expend more energy to:<br /><ul><li>  let go of and delegate tasks</li><li>  compromise for the good of the team</li><li>  join in social events and celebrations</li><li>  make quick decisions</li></ul>When communicating with the C style individual, focus on facts and  details; minimize "pep talk" or emotional language; be patient,  persistent and diplomatic.<br /><br /><b>DiSC Classic Patterns:</b> Objective Thinker, Perfectionist, Practitioner<br /><br /><b>Leadership styles:</b> <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/11/deliberate-leaders-everything-disc/">Deliberate</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/10/humble-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Humble</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2014/01/resolute-leaders-everything-disc/">Resolute</a><br /><br />From our blog: <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/03/understanding-our-c-style-colleagues-and-friends/">Understanding our C-style colleagues and friends</a><br /><br />From our Pinterest site: <a href="http://www.pinterest.com/onlinedisc/C-disc-personality-style/">DiSC C style</a><br /><br /><br /><br /><h1>DiSC Dominance (D) Profile Overview</h1><div class="content"><h2> DiSC profiles explained: D is for Dominance</h2><img alt="D-(2).png" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/Dominance/D-DiSC.png.aspx" style="float: right;" title="D-(2).png" />If you've recently taken the DiSC<sup>Â®</sup> profile test, your report has a more personalized explanation of what  having a D style means for you than what is presented here. But this can  be a handy page to link to and let others learn a bit more about you.  Or you can use it to better understand someone you work or live with.<br /><br />People with the D style place an <b>emphasis on shaping the environment by overcoming opposition to accomplish results.</b><br /><br />A person with a D style<br /><ul><li>  is motivated by winning, competition and success.</li><li>  prioritizes accepting challenge, taking action and achieving immediate results.</li><li>  is described as direct, demanding, forceful, strong willed, driven, and determined, fast-paced, and self-confident.</li><li>  may be limited by lack of concern for others, impatience and open skepticism.</li><li>  may fear being seen as vulnerable or being taken advantage of.</li><li>  values competency, action, concrete results, personal freedom, challenges.</li></ul>Goals:<br /><ul><li>  unique accomplishments</li><li>  new opportunities</li><li>  control of audience</li><li>  independence</li></ul>Will need to expend more energy to:<br /><ul><li>  show patience</li><li>  display sensitivity</li><li>  get into the details</li><li>  allow deliberation</li></ul>When communicating with the D style individuals, give them the bottom  line, be brief, focus your discussion narrowly, avoid making  generalizations, refrain from repeating yourself, and focus on solutions  rather than problems.<br /><br /><b>DiSC Classic Patterns</b>: Developer, Results Orientated, Inspirational and Creative<br /><br /><b>Leadership styles</b>: <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2014/02/commanding-leaders-everything-disc/">Commanding</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2014/01/resolute-leaders-everything-disc/">Resolute</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/06/pioneering-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Pioneering</a><br /><br />From our blog: <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/02/understanding-our-d-style-colleagues-and-friends/">Understanding our D-style colleagues and friends</a><br /><br />From our Pinterest site: <a href="http://www.pinterest.com/onlinedisc/d-disc-personality-style/">DiSC D style</a><br /><h2> Interested in taking the test yourself?</h2>Much more is available to someone who has completed a DiSC profile.&nbsp; Popular DiSC tests include:<br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/disc-classic-2-0/"><i>DiSC Classic 2.0</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-workplace-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Workplace</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-work-of-leaders-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Work of Leaders</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-management-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Management</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <i><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-sales-profile/">Everything DiSC Sales</a></i><br /><h2> What about the other DiSC styles?</h2><ul><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/influence/">Influence (i) DiSC style explained.</a></li><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/steadiness/">Steadiness (S) DiSC style explained.</a></li><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/conscientiousness/">Conscientiousness (C) DiSC style explained.</a></li></ul></div><br /><br /><h1>DiSC Steadiness (S) Profile Overview</h1><h2> <img alt="" src="https://www.discprofile.com/DiscProfile/media/Images/Other/D-i-S-C/S-DiSC.png" style="float: right; margin: auto 5px;" />DiSC profiles explained: S is for Steadiness</h2>If you've recently taken the DiSC<sup>Â®</sup> profile, your report has a  more personalized explanation of what having an S style means for you  than what is presented here. You are welcome to link to this page to  share this information with others. Or you can use it to better  understand someone you work or live with.<br /><br />People with the S style place an<b> emphasis on cooperating with others within existing circumstances to carry out the task.</b>.<br />A person with an S style<br /><ul><li>  is motivated by cooperation, opportunities to help and sincere appreciation</li><li>  prioritizes giving support, collaboration and maintaining stability</li><li>  is described as calm, patient, predictable, deliberate, stable and consistent.</li><li>  may be limited by being indecisive, overly accommodating and tendency to avoid change</li><li>  may fear change, loss of stability and offending others.</li><li>  values loyalty, helping others and security</li></ul>Goals:<br /><ul><li>  personal accomplishments</li><li>  group acceptance</li><li>  power through formal roles and positions of authority</li><li>  maintenance of status quo and controlled environment</li></ul><br /><b>Will need to expend more energy to:</b><br /><ul><li>  quickly adapt to change or unclear expectations</li><li>  multitask</li><li>  promote themselves</li><li>  confront others</li></ul>When communicating with the S style individuals, be personal and  amiable, express your interest in them and what you expect from them,  take time to provide clarification, be polite, and avoid being  confrontational, overly aggressive or rude.<br /><br /><b>DiSC Classic Patterns</b>: Specialist, Achiever, Agent, Investigator<br /><br /><b>Leadership styles:</b> <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/09/inclusive-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Inclusive</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/10/humble-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Humble</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/08/affirming-leaders-and-eveything-disc/">Affirming</a><br /><br />From our blog: <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/03/understanding-our-s-style-colleagues-and-friends/">Understanding our S-style colleagues and friends</a><br /><br />From our Pinterest site: <a href="http://www.pinterest.com/onlinedisc/S-disc-personality-style/">DiSC S style</a><br /><br /><br /><br /><h1>DiSC Influence (i) Profile Overview</h1><div class="content"><h2> DiSC profiles explained: i is for Influence</h2><img alt="I.png" src="https://www.discprofile.com/getattachment/What-is-DiSC/Overview/Influence/i-DiSC.png.aspx" style="float: right;" title="I.png" />If you've recently taken the DiSC<sup>Â®</sup> profile, your report has a more personalized explanation of what having  an i style means for you than what is presented here. But this can be a  fun page to link to and have others learn a bit more about you. Or you  can use it to better understand someone you work or live with.<br /><br />People with the i style place an <b>emphasis on shaping the environment by influencing or persuading others.</b><br /><br />A person with an i style<br /><ul><li>  may be limited by being impulsive and disorganized and having lack of follow-through</li><li>  is described as convincing, magnetic, enthusiastic, warm, trusting and optimistic</li><li>  prioritizes taking action, collaboration, and expressing enthusiasm</li><li>  is motivated by social recognition, group activities, and relationships</li><li>  may fear loss of influence, disapproval and being ignored</li><li>  values coaching and counseling, freedom of expression and democratic relationships</li></ul>Goals<br /><ul><li>  victory with flair</li><li>  friendship and happiness</li><li>  authority and prestige status symbols</li><li>  popularity</li></ul><span style="-webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; background-color: #fffcf8; color: #424c4a; display: inline !important; float: none; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; font-weight: normal; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: 18px; orphans: auto; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; white-space: normal; widows: auto; word-spacing: 0px;">Will need to expend more energy to:</span><br /><ul><li>  follow-through completely</li><li>  research all the facts</li><li>  speak directly and candidly</li><li>  stay focused for long periods</li></ul>When communicating with the i style individual, share your experiences,  allow the i style person time to ask questions and talk themselves,  focus on the positives, avoid overloading them with details, and don't  interrupt them.<br /><br /><b>DiSC Classic Patterns: </b>Promoter, Persuader, Counselor, Appraiser<br /><br /><b>Leadership styles:</b> <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/07/energizing-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Energizing</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/06/pioneering-leaders-and-everything-disc/">Pioneering</a>, <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/08/affirming-leaders-and-eveything-disc/">Affirming</a><br /><br />From our blog: <a href="http://www.discprofiles.com/blog/2013/04/understanding-our-i-style-colleagues-and-friends/">Understanding our i-style colleagues and friends</a><br /><br />From our Pinterest site: <a href="http://www.pinterest.com/onlinedisc/i-disc-personality-style/">DiSC i style</a><br /><h2> Interested in taking the test yourself?</h2>Much more is available to someone who has completed a DiSC profile.&nbsp; Popular DiSC tests include:<br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/disc-classic-2-0/"><i>DiSC Classic 2.0</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-workplace-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Workplace</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-work-of-leaders-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Work of Leaders</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-management-profile/"><i>Everything DiSC Management</i></a><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <i><a href="https://www.discprofile.com/products/everything-disc-sales-profile/">Everything DiSC Sales</a></i><br /><h2> What about the other DiSC styles?</h2><ul><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/dominance/">Dominance (D) DiSC style explained.</a></li><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/conscientiousness/">Conscientiousness (C) DiSC style explained.</a></li><li>  <a href="https://www.discprofile.com/what-is-disc/overview/steadiness/">Steadiness (S) DiSC style explained.</a></li></ul><br /><br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "D"Tendencies seem to be: </strong><br />Convinced, Cocky, Industrious, Hard working, Outspoken, Opinionated, Zealous, Eager<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "I"Tendencies seem to be: </strong><br />Talkative, Verbal<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "S"Tendencies seem to be: </strong><br />Kind, Nice, Caring, Loyal, True Blue, Considerate, Thoughtful<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "C"Tendencies seem to be: </strong><br />Careful,  Cautious, Right, Correct, Contemplative, Thinker, Pondering, Wondering,  Guarded, Masked, Protective, Preparing, Researching, Inquisitive,  Questioning, Original, Creative<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "D"Tendencies are not very: </strong><br />Demanding,  Asserting, Decisive, Sure, Certain, Risk-taking, Courageous,  Persistent, Restless, Relentless, Winner, Competitive, Controlling,  Taking charge, Direct, To the point, Bottom line, Straight-forward<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "I"Tendencies are not very: </strong><br />Hyper, Energetic, Joyful, Jovial, Merry, Cheerful, Peppy, Playful<br /><strong>darrell@darrellwolfe.com's "S"Tendencies are not very: </strong><br />Gentle,  Soft, Humble, Contented, Satisfied, Shy, Mild, Sweet, Tender,  Compassionate, Timid, Soft-spoken, Tolerant, Patient, Hospitable, Enjoys  company<br /><br /><br /><h2>When Both Graphs 1 &amp; 2 Are Generally Different</h2>Your Uniquely You Personality Profile contains basic insights on  how you tend to think, feel, and act from a DISC temperament type   perspective. If your graphs are generally different, you are simply  revealing something contrasting about your behavior. You may be saying   that you think people want you to behave one way in public, but you  sometimes feel you should respond a different way in private. In other   words, if you have a specific high type in Graph 1 and the same type  low in Graph 2, you believe that people expect you to be one way in   public, but not that way in private.<br />   You may also be revealing that you feel that people expect you to  behave one way among fellow employees and associates at work or publicly   outside your more personal and familiar environments (Graph 1), than  you tend to behave either at home under pressure among your closest   friends and relatives or in more familiar environments (Graph 2).   Keep in mind that Graph 1 is the behavior "expected of you" when you   have your guard up and mask on (usually your place of employment or in  casual environments). Graph 2 is "the real you" when you let your   hair down, drop your guard, or take your mask off (usually at home or  among those you know best in more familiar environments).<br />   When both graphs are different, you are either struggling with your  motivations and feelings or you are aware of your challenges and   perhaps handling them well. Having a different configuration in your  two graphs is common and can be very enlightening. It may be a sign that   you are mature and capable of controlling your inner feelings and  natural thoughts. Or, it can mean that you are not even aware of the   struggles within you.<br />   Understanding and adjusting your thoughts, feelings, and actions  using these insights can be very productive and wise. Review and study   your two graphs, keeping in mind the similarities and differences.  Then, learn how to control your motivations rather than letting them   control you.<br /><h2> Your <em>LOW "I"</em> on Graph 1 and <em>HIGH "I"</em> on Graph 2</h2>You  have a Low "I" on Graph 1 and a High "I" on Graph 2. This usually means  you think people expect you to be less     influencing and impressing than what you really tend to be. It may  indicate that you are more entertaining and enthusiastic than     what you show and act publicly. This could explain why you can be  more outgoing in private, but not as inspiring or talkative in     public.<br />    The degree to which you are outgoing is shown by how high or low  your "I" is on Graphs 1 or 2. In other words, if your "I" is closer    to the bottom of your graph, you will be less influential and  impressing than when your "I" is closer to or higher than the midline.  If    your "I" is just below the midline, you will be a little less  interested in people, but not as much as when your "I" is higher than  the    midline. The lower your "I," the less outgoing and involved you tend  to be.<br />    You think people want you to be less active and extroverted in  groups, while privately with your closest friends, you may be more    people-oriented. You are more expressive among dearest acquaintances  than you are among those you don't know as well. This doesn't mean    you are not friendly with others. It may indicate that you are more  comfortable entertaining or impressing those closest to you than    you are with strangers or those you don't know as well.<br />    Your inspiring interest in people causes you to be very motivated  to impress smaller and more intimate groups. At work or among     those who are not your dearest friends and relatives, you may not be  as expressive or excited. You seem to be quicker to speak in private    when it is not expected of you, and slower to communicate when you  are among strangers.<br />    You are not as driven to look good in public, but you seem to be  more talkative and more excited in private. This sometimes causes    others to wonder why you are not so friendly outside your home or  familiar environments. They may wonder why you tend to be more shy    when in large groups or in public. You often give mixed or confusing  messages. People sometimes think you don't like or care about them    because you seem interested only in your closest acquaintances.<br />    Be more interested in looking good in a crowd. Be as enthusiastic  publicly as you can be privately. Learn to be just as friendly and    encouraging among those who are not your close friends or relatives.<br />    In summary, your low "I" in Graph 1 and high "I" in Graph 2 means  you are more outgoing and friendly among close friends and relatives    than you are at work or in unfamiliar environments.<br /><br /><ul></ul></div>
